name: California Science Center, Los Angeles
total_construction_area: 385,000 sq ft (Phase I & II); 200,000 sq ft (Phase III expansion under construction)
building_height: 200 ft (Samuel Oschin Air and Space Center); 88 ft (Rotunda)
concept&appearance: Designed by ZGF Architects, the center's architecture represents a 'fusion of past and future.' The original main building features a preserved historic Beaux-Arts brick-and-terra-cotta facade from the 1912 State Exposition Building on the north side, while the new entry plaza and south facade showcase massive, modern geometric forms and a cylindrical rotunda with a steel exoskeleton. The Phase III Samuel Oschin Air and Space Center (nearing completion) adds a unique identity with a curvilinear design clad in stainless steel, inspired by the aerodynamic, fluid geometry of the Space Shuttle Endeavour's fuselage and wings.
permanent_exhibitions:
1. **World of Life**: Explores the commonalities of all living things (from single-celled organisms to humans) through five life processes: Energy, Supply, Control, Defense, and Reproduction. 
2. **Creative World**: Examines the man-made environment and the consequences of human innovation, featuring hands-on exhibits on construction, transportation (solar cars), and communication. 
3. **Ecosystems**: A major wing integrating over 200 live plant and animal species with hands-on science exhibits across 11 immersive environments, including a 188,000-gallon Kelp Forest tank, a Desert Flash Flood, and Polar/River zones. 
4. **Fire! Science & Safety**: Casa Safe, a multimedia exhibit teaching fire prevention and safety. 
5. **Air and Space Exhibits**: Historically housed in the SKETCH Foundation Gallery, these artifacts (including capsules and aircraft) are transitioning to the new Samuel Oschin Air and Space Center, which will feature the Space Shuttle Endeavour in a vertical 'ready-to-launch' stack.
central_hall: The museum features two key central spaces: the **Rotunda** and the **Science Court**. The Rotunda is a cylindrical volume 100 feet in diameter and 88 feet high, featuring a steel exoskeleton and a skylight with dichroic glass that projects shifting colors; it houses 'The Aerial,' a sculpture of 1,578 gold and palladium leaf spheres. The Science Court is a towering, wedge-shaped glass atrium (80 feet high) that serves as the main lobby, visually connecting the modern south entrance with the historic north facade and Rose Garden.
special_effects_theaters: IMAX Theater: Features a 7-story high (approx. 90 feet wide) screen, utilizing IMAX with Laser technology for 4K 3D projection and a 12-channel surround sound system. It is capable of showing educational documentaries and blockbuster films.
science_popularization_activities: Offers comprehensive educational programming including **Hands-On Science Camp** (week-long summer classes for Pre-K to 8th grade), **Community Teen Interns** (work-based learning for local high schoolers), **Young Curators** (middle school program), and **Virtual Field Trips**. The center also operates the **Science Center School**, an on-site charter elementary school emphasizing science, math, and technology.
